concept.name: Modular arithmetic
concept.definition: A way of counting where the numbers wrap back to the start once they reach a fixed limit, the way the hours on a clock jump from 12 back to 1.

audience: a curious adult with no domain background
atomic_reason: One short clock-face figure of a few frames carries the whole idea: a hand sweeps past 12, wraps back to 1, and lands on the answer. The only thing it leans on is reading a clock and basic counting, which the audience already knows, so no sub-figure is needed.
